items.title: Standardize before distance-based clustering
items.avoid: Feeding raw mixed-unit features into Euclidean hierarchical clustering.
items.prefer: Apply `StandardScaler` or domain-appropriate normalization, then cluster on the scaled matrix.
items.why: Distance-based merges are only meaningful when dimensions contribute comparably to dissimilarity.
items.example: RFM clustering: scale recency (days), frequency (counts), and revenue (dollars) to zero mean and unit variance before `linkage(..., method='ward')`.

items.title: Inspect the dendrogram before fixing k
items.avoid: Setting `n_clusters=3` because three is a round number without visual or metric support.
items.prefer: Plot the dendrogram, look for large vertical gaps (long branches before a merge), and cross-check with silhouette or domain constraints.
items.why: Large gaps suggest merges that combine genuinely dissimilar groups; small gaps suggest arbitrary splits.
items.example: If the largest jump in cophenetic distance occurs between heights 2.1 and 4.8, try cuts near 3.5 and compare cluster sizes and silhouette.

items.title: Match linkage to geometry and metric
items.avoid: Defaulting to Ward on cosine embeddings or categorical-heavy mixed data without thought.
items.prefer: Use Ward with Euclidean on standardized numeric features; use average or complete linkage with cosine/Manhattan when that matches the data geometry.
items.why: Linkage assumes a specific notion of cluster dissimilarity; mismatches distort the hierarchy.
items.example: Text embeddings: `linkage(pdist(E, metric='cosine'), method='average')` instead of Ward.
